data.description:
## Description

Write a failing RED integration test for the `ticket classify` subcommand before the implementation exists.

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test-ticket-classify.sh (new)
- .test-index (modify — append to `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket` and `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh` entries)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-ticket-classify.sh exists and is executable (chmod +x)
- [ ] Test invokes `ticket classify <id>` through the dispatcher (not classify-task.sh directly)
- [ ] Test uses a frozen fixture (static ticket JSON, not live ticket data) to ensure deterministic scoring
- [ ] Test asserts JSON output contains required keys: model, subagent, class, complexity, priority
- [ ] Test asserts expected field values for the frozen fixture (snapshot assertion — not just key presence)
- [ ] Test asserts that calling with a missing poetry env emits a diagnostic to stderr and exits non-zero (not silent failure)
- [ ] Tests FAIL (RED) before implementation task T2 runs
- [ ] .test-index updated to add tests/scripts/test-ticket-classify.sh to `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket` and `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh` entries

## Red Marker
tests/scripts/test-ticket-classify.sh [test_classify_returns_json_through_dispatcher]

## Notes
classify-task.sh uses TICKET_CMD env var to call `ticket show` for each ID. Tests can inject a mock TICKET_CMD (same pattern as test-validate-issues.sh) to return fixture ticket JSON without a live ticket repo. classify-task.sh outputs a JSON array even for single ticket input. The test fixture should be a simple task ticket JSON with keywords in the title/description that produce a deterministic classification result.
data.title: [RED] Write failing integration test for ticket classify dispatcher
